opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was just watching TV and suddenly my heart felt like a machine gun, my chest felt tight, and I got incredibly dizzy.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on completely out of the blue about 45 minutes ago and my heart is still racing really fast — I feel like I might pass out if I stand up.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 45 minutes ago, out of nowhere while sitting watching TV — like a switch was flipped
symptom_narrative.character: regular and extremely fast, pounding like a machine gun, with a strong pounding feeling in my neck
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really frightening, the worst racing heart I've ever had
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, no let-up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up makes me feel like I'm going to faint
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: tight chest, feeling faint/nearly blacking out when I stand, a bit breathless just walking to the kitchen, sweaty and very anxious
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I don't remember anything like this happening before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm sitting very still on the sofa because I get too dizzy to stand up

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally healthy, no heart problems that I know of

family_history_as_reported.narrative: no family history of sudden death or heart problems that I'm aware of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; had two large double espressos earlier today
social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: drinks a fair bit of strong coffee

objective_data_offered.value: when I put my fingers on my wrist it's way too fast to count and it feels steady/regular
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-palpated by an anxious patient, not a device reading — cannot confirm rate or regularity

objective_data_offered.value: two large double espressos this afternoon (empty cups still on the coffee table)
